title: Race-weekend holdout CV plus OOF target encoding
summary: Rows from the same race share hidden context like weather, tyre nomination, pit-loss, and pit-wave timing, so random row CV can overestimate generalization.
rationale: Rows from the same race share hidden context like weather, tyre nomination, pit-loss, and pit-wave timing, so random row CV can overestimate generalization. High-cardinality identifiers such as Driver and Driver x Race x Compound likely contain signal, but only if encoded leakage-safely.
implementation_hint: Score experiments with `LeaveOneGroupOut` or `LeavePGroupsOut` on `Race_Year`, and build only fold-local target encodings for combos like `Driver`, `Driver x Compound`, `Driver x Race`, `Race x Compound`, `Stint x Compound`, and lap buckets. Compare against CatBoost native categorical handling with ordered statistics.
expected_effect: Cleaner local CV and stronger generalization from categorical priors that are richer than raw frequency encoding.
risk: Public score may not rise if leaderboard/test races are very similar to train races; OOF encoding also adds implementation complexity and variance if category counts are small.
